title: Binomial Distribution: Calculating Mean and Variance
description: A flowchart demonstrating the step-by-step process to calculate the mean (expected value) and variance for a binomial distribution, showing the relationship between its parameters (n, p) and the resulting formulas.
elements: Binomial Parameters: n (number of trials), p (probability of success)
Define Random Variable: X ~ Binomial(n, p)
Variance Formula: σ² = n * p * (1 - p)
relationships: n and p are the inputs for the distribution
The mean is calculated directly from n and p
The variance is calculated using n, p, and (1-p)
The standard deviation is derived from the variance
